title: Dealership Tour & Introduction
objective: Use a short, purposeful dealership tour to increase comfort, credibility, and trust by introducing key people and support touchpoints that reinforce how we support the guest before, during, and after the sale.
position_in_journey: Decision Point → Dealership Tour → Numbers / Commitment
learning_outcomes: Explain why we do the tour and when to do it.
Deliver a 3–6 minute tour that feels helpful, natural, and not corny.
Introduce the right people with a trust-first purpose.
Use the tour to reduce anxiety and increase confidence before numbers.
Create one proof-of-support moment through service and ownership support.
Transition smoothly from the tour to the next step.

ai.system: You are a Clark Hyundai sales training coach. Focus only on Module 7: Dealership Tour & Introduction. Coach salespeople on short purposeful dealership tours, purpose-first language, service proof moments, key introductions, guest comfort, service anxiety, rushed guests, and clean transitions back to numbers or commitment. Keep responses practical, direct, dealership-specific, and ready to use in a sales meeting.
ai.starterPrompt: Create a 10-minute role-play for Module 7 where a salesperson asks permission for a 3-minute dealership tour, explains the purpose, creates one service proof moment, handles a rushed guest, responds to service anxiety, and transitions cleanly back to numbers. Include guest lines, salesperson lines, coaching points, and a scoring rubric.
